MINI MINUTES FOR OCT-26-2020 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, RESPONDENTS SBM SITE SERVICES, SBM MANAGEMENT SERVICES' MOTION TO DISMISS THIS ACTION BASED UPON CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 583.310 IS DENIED. SECTION 583.310 PROVIDES THAT "[A]N ACTION SHALL BE BROUGHT TO TRIAL WITHIN FIVE YEARS AFTER THE ACTION IS COMMENCED AGAINST THE DEFENDANT." IN THIS CASE, PETITIONER COMMENCED THIS ACTION ON SEPTEMBER 4, 2015; THUS, BUT FOR THE COVID-19 PANDEMIC, THE FIVE-YEAR PERIOD WOULD HAVE EXPIRED ON SEPTEMBER 4, 2020. HOWEVER, EMERGENCY RULE 10(A) OF THE CALIFORNIA RULES OF COURT, WHICH WAS ADOPTED BY THE JUDICIAL COUNCIL ON APRIL 6, 2020, PROVIDES, "NOTWITHSTANDING ANY OTHER LAW, INCLUDING CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 583.310, FOR ALL CIVIL ACTIONS FILED ON OR BEFORE APRIL 6, 2020, THE TIME IN WHICH TO BRING THE ACTION TO TRIAL IS EXTENDED BY SIX MONTHS FOR A TOTAL TIME OF FIVE YEARS AND SIX MONTHS." THUS, THE MANDATORY PERIOD HAS YET TO RUN. PETITIONER CITES NO AUTHORITY SUPPORTING HER CONTENTION THAT THE MANDATORY DISMISSAL STATUTES DO NOT APPLY TO ACTIONS TO CONFIRM ARBITRATION AWARDS. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM.

calendar.matters:
MOTION TO CONFIRM ARBITRATION AWARD
Off Calendar Dec-02-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
HEARING RE: PETITIONER NUBIA OROZCO'S AMENDED MOTION TO CONFIRM ARBITRATION AWARD.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Petitioner Nubia Orozco's motion to confirm arbitration award is granted. Respondents did not file a petition to vacate or correct the arbitrator's award within 100 days after the date of service of the award. (Code Civ. Proc. sec. 1288.) Accordingly, the Court is required to confirm the award. (Code Civ. Proc. sec. 1286; Soni v. SimpleLayers, Inc. (2019) 42 Cal.App.5th 1071, 1085; Law Offices of David S. Karton v. Segreto (2009) 176 Cal.App.4th 1, 8.) Rather than file a petition to vacate or correct the award, Respondents filed a "response" arguing that Petitioner lacked standing, and subsequently filed a motion for judgment on the pleadings on the same ground, which the Court denied by order dated March 7, 2016. Respondents' opposition to the petition to confirm the award principally relies upon the same ground. However, Respondents did not seek reconsideration of the Court's ruling on their motion for judgment on the pleadings, and the Court declines to revisit that ruling.

The arbitrator ordered that Petitioner be "reinstated to her former position" and that she "be made whole with back pay and benefits, and without loss of seniority," and retained jurisdiction "in the event of a dispute over this remedy." The parties have not been able to agree upon the amount of back pay to which Petitioner is entitled; accordingly, that issue must be submitted to the arbitrator for resolution. (See Printing Specialties & Paper Products Union v. Litton Financial Printing Co. (1982) 129 Cal.App.3d 100, 106 [where amount of award has not been determined, "[i]f the arbitrator retains jurisdiction, the proper and practical course is to remand those issues to him for computation and identification"]; see also Mossman v. City of Oakdale (2009) 170 Cal.App.4th 83, 91-92 [where arbitrator's award required that employee be made whole and parties' agreement granted arbitrator continuing jurisdiction to resolve issues regarding the implementation of any remedy granted, the proper remedy was for the matter to be to the arbitrator for further clarification of the award].)

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/EPS)

Defendants Sbm Site Services And Sbm Management Services Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Action
Off Calendar Oct-26-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Scott Nakama of the law offices of Ladva Law Firm (snakama@ladvalaw.com) appears as counsel for plaintiff/defendant/moving/opposing party

Attorney, Nicole Baarts of the law offices of Seyfarth Law..(nbaarts@seyfarth.com) appears as counsel for defendants/moving parties, SBM Site Services, SBM Management Services

No other appearance is made in this matter

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides, on record as reflected in the notes of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

RESPONDENTS SBM SITE SERVICES, SBM MANAGEMENT SERVICES' Motion To Dismiss this action based upon Code of Civil Procedure section 583.310 is denied. Section 583.310 provides that "[a]n action shall be brought to trial within five years after the action is commenced against the defendant." In this case, petitioner commenced this action on September 4, 2015; thus, but for the COVID-19 pandemic, the five-year period would have expired on September 4, 2020. However, Emergency rule 10(a) of the California Rules of Court, which was adopted by the Judicial Council on April 6, 2020, provides, "Notwithstanding any other law, including Code of Civil Procedure section 583.310, for all civil actions filed on or before April 6, 2020, the time in which to bring the action to trial is extended by six months for a total time of five years and six months." Thus, the mandatory period has yet to run. Petitioner cites no authority supporting her contention that the mandatory dismissal statutes do not apply to actions to confirm arbitration awards.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings Regarding Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award
Off Calendar Mar-07-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. JOHN HENNING, ESQ. of Ladva Law Firm, Tel No. 415-296-8844 appearing for the Petitioner;
Atty. NICOLE BOLSON BAARTS, ESQ. of Seyfarth Shaw LLP, Tel No. 415-544-1055 appearing for Respondents SBM Site Services and SBM Management Services.

Matter argued and submitted by counsel. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. RESPONDENTS SBM SITE SERVICES AND SBM MANAGEMENT SERVICES' Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings Regarding Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award is denied. Melander v. Hughes Aircraft Co. (1987) 194 Cal.App.3d 542 does not require granting this motion. Melander held that when an employee grievance is arbitrated under the terms of a collective bargaining agreement between an employer and a union, the employee does not have standing to petition to vacate the arbitration award. Melander distinguished Sublett v. Henry's etc. Lunch (1942) 21 Cal. 2d 273 which held that an employee may enforce the terms of a union contract as a third party beneficiary. Because an unconfirmed arbitration award is a contract (CCP 1287.6), what plaintiff is seeking to do in this case is much more akin to what was permitted in Sublett than what was precluded in Melander. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)
